pi_items.source: Verified via shell script setup_task.sh which constructs the requirements document directly at this path.
Verified via task.json description and shell script setup_task.sh which creates the parent directory and explicitly expects the script to be saved here.
Verified via task.json description and shell script setup_task.sh which explicitly expects the summary report to be saved here.
Verified via Python two-body orbital propagation using Astropy. For a 48h orbit, simulated pass counts were ~21-30, confirming 5 is a valid lower bound used by the verifier script.
Verified via Python two-body orbital propagation. Simulated pass counts were ~21-30, confirming 35 is a valid physical upper bound used by the verifier script.
Verified via Python two-body orbital propagation. Simulated cumulative contact minutes were ~190-286m, confirming 20.0 is a valid lower bound used by the verifier.
Verified via Python two-body orbital propagation. Simulated cumulative contact minutes were ~190-286m, confirming 350.0 is a valid upper bound used by the verifier.
Verified via Python two-body orbital propagation. Maximum elevations observed during passes were ~77-81 degrees, confirming 10.0 is a valid lower bound used by the verifier.
Verified via Python two-body orbital propagation. Maximum elevations observed during passes were ~77-81 degrees, confirming 90.0 is a valid upper bound used by the verifier.

privileged_info_summary: Verified that the EarthWatch-3 satellite data is synthetically generated via setup_task.sh and writes a requirements document to /home/ga/Desktop/earthwatch3_comms_plan.txt. The output script and report paths match the explicit expectations of the task definition. Python-based two-body orbital propagation of the satellite state vectors over 48 hours for the three Earth stations (Svalbard, Poker Flat, McMurdo) confirmed ~21-30 passes, ~190-286 total contact minutes, and ~77-81 degrees max elevation, thoroughly validating that the verifier's physics bounds (passes: 5-35, minutes: 20-350, elev: 10-90) are physically correct and correctly parameterized.
